summary: Soundboard recording of decent quality captures a sloppy but high‑energy 1984 show, with standout Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s and West L.A. Fadeaway amid vocal missteps.
review: The SBD tape is well‑transferred – clear enough to hear the band despite occasional echo and a bit of distortion in the Space/Other One section. Musically the night is uneven: the first set suffers from lyrical flubs and loose playing, while the second set delivers classic moments such as Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s, a strong Terrapin, and an inspired West L.A. Fadeaway. Overall the performance is typical of ’84 – high intensity with plenty of slip‑ups.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers note a solid soundboard transfer (recording quality good) but describe the performance as uneven; the weighted average of show vs. recording points to a mid‑to‑high 3‑star range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great tape transfer, generally clear and balanced; occasional echoy sections and minor distortion on the Space/Other One transition, but overall listenable.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set feels loose with missed lyrics and vocal strain; second set picks up intensity, delivering strong jams and classic transitions despite occasional lyrical mishaps.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed: praised for solid tone when not over‑processed, but noted to miss cues, stumble on lyrics (Stella, Terrapin) and seem out of sync with the band.
band_member_comments.Bob: Frequent vocal slips – flubbed Truckin', improvised lines in The Other One, and general struggle with lyric delivery throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's keyboard work noted as solid musically but his vocal mix was criticized for being too low or over‑reverbed.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally competent; one reviewer mentions drums cut out on a transition, otherwise no major complaints.
